session.assistant: Now let me read the rest of the `trigger_workflow` method in the fake to see if there's a place to inject the error:
session.tool_result:
249→ def trigger_workflow( 250→ self, *, repo_root: Path, workflow: str, inputs: dict[str, str], ref: str | None 251→ ) -> str: 252→ """Record workflow trigger in mutation tracking list. 253→ 254→ Note: In production, trigger_workflow() generates a distinct_id internally 255→ and adds it to the inputs. Tests should verify the workflow was called 256→ with expected inputs; the distinct_id is an internal implementation detail. 257→ 258→ Also creates a WorkflowRun entry so get_workflow_run() can find it. 259→ This simulates the real behavior where triggering a workflow creates a run. 260→ 261→ Returns: 262→ A fake run ID for testing 263→ """ 264→ self._triggered_workflows.append((workflow, inputs)) 265→ run_id = "1234567890" 266→ # Create a WorkflowRun entry so get_workflow_run() can find it 267→ # Use branch_name from inputs if available 268→ branch = inputs.get("branch_name", "main") 269→ triggered_run = WorkflowRun( 270→ run_id=run_id, 271→ status="queued", 272→ conclusion=None, 273→ branch=branch, 274→ head_sha="abc123", 275→ node_id=f"WFR_{run_id}", 276→ ) 277→ # Prepend to list so it's found first (most recent) 278→ self._workflow_runs.insert(0, triggered_run)
... [15 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now I need to read the relevant testing docs. Let me check the gateway fake testing exemplar and the architecture doc for gateway ABC implementation:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing tests for gateway fakes that return discriminated unions" 5→ - "deciding whether mutation tracking should occur on error paths" 6→ - "implementing a new fake gateway method with error injection" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a fake gateway without constructor-injected error configuration" 9→ warning: "Fakes must accept error variants at construction time (e.g., push_to_remote_error=PushError(...)) to enable failure injection in tests." 10→ - action: "using truthiness checks or .success on discriminated union results in tests" 11→ warning: "Always use isinstance() for type narrowing. Never check bool(result) or result.success — these bypass the type system." 12→ - action: "tracking mutations before checking error configuration in a fake method" 13→ warning: "Decide deliberately: should this operation track even on failure? push_to_remote skips tracking on error (nothing happened), pull_rebase always tracks (the attempt matters). Match the real operation's semantics." 14→--- 15→ 16→# Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 17→ 18→This document captures the cross-cutting design decisions in fake gateway testing — specifically, the three patterns that every fake test must exercise and the subtle tracking-on-error decision that differs between operations. 19→ 20→## Why Fakes Need Their Own Tests 21→ 22→Fakes are production code for tests. A broken fake silently produces false-positive test results across the entire test suite. The `tests/unit/fakes/` directory exists to verify that fakes honor their ABC contracts and that their error-injection and mutation-tracking machinery works correctly. 23→ 24→<!-- Source: tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py --> 25→ 26→See `test_fake_git_remote_ops.py` in `tests/unit/fakes/` for the canonical exemplar. 27→ 28→## Three Required Test Categories Per Operation 29→ 30→Every fake method that returns a discriminated union needs three test categories:
... [35 lines omitted] ...
35→| **Error injection** | Constructor-configured error is returned | Proves failure paths work without modifying fake internals | 40→## The Tracking-on-Error Decision 42→The most subtle design choice in fake implementation is whether mutation tracking should occur when an error is configured. This is not arbitrary — it follows the real operation's semantics: 44→| Operation | Tracks on error? | Rationale | 52→Compare `FakeGitRemoteOps.push_to_remote()` (early-returns error before tracking) with `FakeGitRemoteOps.pull_rebase()` (tracks first, then checks error) in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py`. 54→**When implementing a new fake method**, ask: "If the real operation fails, did a side effect still occur?" If yes, track before checking the error. If no, check the error first and skip tracking.

1→--- 2→title: Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-15 18:50 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding or modifying methods in any gateway ABC interface (Git, GitHub, Graphite)" 7→ - "implementing new gateway operations" 8→ - "composing one gateway inside another (e.g., GitHub composing GitHubIssues)" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "adding a new method to Git ABC" 11→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 12→ - action: "adding a new method to GitHub ABC" 13→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 14→ - action: "adding a new method to Graphite ABC" 15→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 16→ - action: "removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC" 17→ warning: "Must remove from 5 places simultaneously: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py. Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites to use subgateway property. Verify with grep across packages." 18→ - action: "adding subprocess.run or run_subprocess_with_context calls to a gateway real.py file" 19→ warning: "Must add integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_*.py. Real gateway methods with subprocess calls need tests that verify the actual subprocess behavior." 20→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(|run_subprocess_with_context\\(" 21→ - action: "making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data" 22→ warning: "Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern." 23→ - action: "using subprocess.run with git command outside of a gateway" 24→ warning: "Use the Git gateway instead. Direct subprocess calls bypass testability (fakes) and dry-run support. The Git ABC (erk_shared.gateway.git.abc.Git) likely already has a method for this operation. Only use subprocess directly in real.py gateway implementations." 25→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(\\s*\\[.*[\"']git" 26→ - action: "changing gateway return type to discriminated union" 27→ warning: "Verify all 5 implementations import the new types. Missing imports in abc.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, or printing.py break the gateway pattern." 28→ - action: "designing error handling for a new gateway method" 29→ warning: "Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section." 30→ - action: "adding a new parameter to a gateway ABC method"
... [15 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "All 5 implementations must be updated (ABC, Real, Fake, DryRun, Printing). Fake may accept but not track new parameters when assertion is not needed for tests."

Now I understand the patterns. I need to:
1. Add `trigger_workflow_error: str | None = None` constructor parameter to FakeGitHub 2. Make `trigger_workflow()` check it and raise `RuntimeError` 3. Write tests in the dispatch test file
However, I should consider: do I really need to modify the FakeGitHub? The `trigger_workflow` ABC method raises `RuntimeError` (not a discriminated union), which means the error injection pattern could also follow the `review_threads_rate_limited` pattern (which raises RuntimeError). Let me look at how that's done:
session.tool_result:
67- pr_update_should_succeed: bool = True, 68- pr_review_threads: dict[int, list[PRReviewThread]] | None = None, 69- pr_check_runs: dict[int, list[PRCheckRun]] | None = None, 70: review_threads_rate_limited: bool = False, 71- resolve_thread_failures: set[str] | None = None, 72- pr_diff_error: str | None = None, 73- workflow_runs_error: str | None = None, -- 105- merge_should_succeed: Whether merge_pr() should succeed (default True) 106- pr_update_should_succeed: Whether PR updates should succeed (default True) 107- pr_review_threads: Mapping of pr_number -> list[PRReviewThread] 108: review_threads_rate_limited: Whether get_pr_review_threads() should raise 109- RuntimeError simulating GraphQL rate limit 110- resolve_thread_failures: Set of thread IDs that should fail when resolved 111- pr_diff_error: If set, get_pr_diff() raises RuntimeError with this message. -- 150- self._merge_should_succeed = merge_should_succeed 151- self._pr_update_should_succeed = pr_update_should_succeed 152- self._pr_review_threads = pr_review_threads or {} 153: self._review_threads_rate_limited = review_threads_rate_limited 154- self._resolve_thread_failures = resolve_thread_failures or set() 155- self._pr_diff_error = pr_diff_error 156- self._workflow_runs_error = workflow_runs_error -- 876- Applies any resolutions that happened during the test, then filters 877- and sorts the results. 878- 879: Raises RuntimeError if review_threads_rate_limited is True, simulating 880- a GraphQL API rate limit error. 881- """
... [4 lines omitted] ...
883- raise RuntimeError("GraphQL API RATE_LIMIT exceeded")
session.assistant:

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8589#issuecomment-3981559953" }, { "id": 3981560571, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 17:39:39 PT

No tripwire violations detected. The error handling changes follow documented CLI patterns correctly.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- **CLI Error Handling Anti-Patterns** → loaded docs/learned/cli/error-handling-antipatterns.md

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches
All universal tripwire patterns checked:
✅ bare subprocess.run with check=True - None found
✅ adding methods to Gateway ABCs - None found
✅ raising exceptions for expected failures - None found
✅ Path.home() direct usage - None found
✅ os.chdir() calls - None found
✅ time module imports - None found
✅ I/O in __init__ - None found
✅ gh pr diff --name-only usage - None found
✅ session IDs via environment variables - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches
✅ **Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures** - COMPLIANT

The code correctly CATCHES RuntimeError from the gateway layer and converts it to UserFacingCliError with actionable messages. This follows the migration pattern documented in error-handling-antipatterns.md:
- Detects known error patterns (404/Not Found)
- Provides actionable remediation (erk init capability add command)
- Preserves exception chain with `from e`

### Violations Summary
None.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 17:39:39 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Error handling follows documented CLI patterns correctly.

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 17:40:19 PT

No violations detected. Code follows dignified Python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Exception handling - Compliant error boundary with context
✅ Exception chaining - Proper B904 pattern with `from e`
✅ String inspection - Valid third-party API compatibility pattern
✅ Error transformation - User-facing error message at CLI boundary

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`: No violations

---

- **2026-03-01 17:40:19 PT**: Error boundary pattern review - compliant. Exception handling at CLI level with proper context addition and chaining is allowed. String inspection for "404"/"Not Found" is acceptable for third-party API compatibility.

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.assistant:
## All PR Comments Addressed
Total comments: 2 actionable review threads + 2 automated bot discussion comments Batches: 2 (combined into 1 commit) Commits: 1 (`40844278e`)
### Changes Made
**Batch 1 — Local Fix** (`dispatch_cmd.py:276`): - Replaced `str(e)` with `e.args[0]` for direct error message access
**Batch 2 — Test Coverage** (`dispatch_cmd.py:268`): - Added `trigger_workflow_error` injection parameter to `FakeGitHub` - Added `test_dispatch_workflow_not_found_shows_install_hint` — verifies 404/Not Found triggers install instructions - Added `test_dispatch_workflow_generic_runtime_error` — verifies generic RuntimeError shows error message
All review threads resolved. All discussion comments replied to.
### Next steps 1. Push changes: - **Graphite repos**: `gt submit` (or `gt ss`) - **Plain git repos**: `git push` - If push is rejected (non-fast-forward): Run `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` to resolve. 2. Wait for CI to pass 3. Request re-review if needed
